slides.headline: Brickline turns site photos into signed-off inspections
slides.notes: One sentence, then stop. Let them ask how.

slides.headline: Every inspection costs a site 4.1 hours
lines: Measured across our pilot sites: 4.1 hours idle per inspection, $8,400 per failed one.
US rework bill: $47B a year (FMI, public).

slides.headline: Photo to signed inspection in 11 minutes
lines: Median across 31 live sites: 11 minutes, 92% first-pass acceptance.
A national builder cut idle time 78% in month one.
slides.notes: The builder stays unnamed until written permission lands.

slides.headline: Inspectors retire faster than they are replaced
lines: Vision models now read site photos better than tired humans at hour 9.
What compounds for us: every accepted inspection trains the next one.

slides.headline: 880K active US sites, $1,500/site per month
lines: Bottoms-up from our live pricing, not top-down TAM.

slides.headline: Why not the platform? Inspectors sign, platforms cannot
lines: Incumbents sell cameras; the default path is manual reinspection.
Our wedge survives platform gravity: the signed record is the product, not the model.

slides.headline: Shipped: capture app, review queue, audit trail
lines: In production since March. 92% of inspections close without a site visit.

slides.headline: $1,500/site per month, 78% gross margin
lines: Measured on current sites. Net revenue retention compounds as sites add inspectors; modeled at scale ~$140K ARR per account.

slides.headline: Failed a site-software startup; kept the scars
lines: Second time building for inspectors. First time the inspectors asked us to.

slides.headline: $2.5M for 1,000 sites in 18 months
lines: Milestones attested in our data room: site count, margin, audit-trail certification.
